'''bapara''' is an [[Aelaki]] [[Nouns|noun]] meaning "[[Wikt:pycnoconidium|pycnoconidium]] (auto-generated)". It has inherent '''male''' gender.

== Overview ==
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Root consonants
| b-p-r
|-
! Word class
| [[Noun]]
|-
! Gloss
| [[Wikt:pycnoconidium|pycnoconidium]] (auto-generated)
|-
! Inherent gender
| male
|-
! Citation form
| bapara
|}

== Inflected forms ==
{| class="wikitable sortable"
! Gender.Number !! 1st !! 2nd !! 3rd !! 4th
|-
| [[male]].[[collective]] || bæpæræth || bæpæræj || bæpæræsh || bæpæræ
|-
| [[male]].[[plural]] || bapaparath || bapaparaj || bapaparash || bapapara
|-
| [[male]].[[singular]] || baparath || baparaj || baparash || bapara
|-
| [[male]].[[zero]] || bapafrafth || bapafrafj || bapafrafsh || bapafraf
|}

''16 forms generated.''

== Case paradigm ==
''Shown for male singular.''
{| class="wikitable"
! Case !! 1st !! 2nd !! 3rd !! 4th
|-
| [[Agent]] || baparatha || baparaja || baparasha || baparaka
|-
| [[Patient]] || baparath || baparaj || baparash || bapara
|-
| [[Possessive]] || baparathal || baparajal || baparashal || baparakal
|-
| [[Instrumental]] || baparaathatha || baparaajaja || baparaashasha || baparaaka
|-
| [[Dative]] || baparathan || baparajan || baparashan || baparakan
|-
| [[Speaker]] || baparathaoro || baparajaoro || baparashaoro || baparakaoro
|}

== See also ==
* [[Aelaki Lexicon]]

[[Category:Aelaki nouns]]
[[Category:Aelaki male nouns]]
[[Category:Aelaki vocabulary]]
[[Category:Aelaki lemmas]]
[[Category:Word pages]]
[[Category:Words c2e237e]]
{{wordpage|d176386}}
